content: We can reframe autonomous curiosity-driven agents to treat failed trajectories not as wasted data, but as a built-in self-supervised source of intrinsic reward that amplifies curiosity beyond standard ICM/RND prediction error. This creates a "failure-seeking curious agent" that actively prioritizes exploring under-tested regions where past attempts fell short, rather than just exploring unfamiliar state spaces.
source_topics: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
